quoteLast: the counsellor, who had a habit of speaking in a very summary manner, resumed in these words,—
title: A Winter Amid the Ice, and Other Thrilling Stories
author: Verne, Jules, 1828-1905

quoteFirst: George Fellows, and Amos Denison. Just before
quoteLast: , six volunteers from Mystic, Jeremiah Holmes, Jeremiah Haley, Ebenezer Denison, Isaac Denison, and Nathaniel Clift, reached the place, on foot, and ran immediately to help to operate the gun in the battery."...
title: The Defence of Stonington (Connecticut) Against a British Squadron, August 9th to 12th, 1814

quoteLast: supper was ready, he kept the operator occupied. He sent to the _Palladium_ a thoroughly plausible story giving prominence to the Appleweight case and laying stress on Governor Dangerfield's vigorous personality and high sense of official responsibility.
title: The Little Brown Jug at Kildare
author: Nicholson, Meredith, 1866-1947; Flagg, James Montgomery, 1877-1960 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: They were unkind enough to declare that our good relative's story was true enough up to the time of his stopping at the "Duke's Head," but that at that point he had quitted the limits of strict veracity. They pretended to have the authority of the landlord of that highly respectable inn for the fact, that Uncle Joe, soon after
quoteLast: , came in and had, not one glass, but three good "stiff" tumblers of brandy-and-water before resuming his journey. They further maintained that he had gone on merrily for a while after this, but that it had had sufficient effect upon him to have rendered it very desirable that he should have kept in the road instead of following the pathway above it.
title: Uncle Joe's Stories
author: Brabourne, Edward Hugessen Knatchbull-Hugessen, Baron, 1829-1893; Griset, Ernest Henry, 1844-1907 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: Opposite El Caney, General Lawton was in command of about seven thousand Americans. The fight here began at half-past
quoteLast: , but the American artillery was placed at too great a distance to be very effective. The result was a long and galling exchange of rifle firing, which is apt to prove trying to raw troops.
title: The Path of Empire: A Chronicle of the United States as a World Power
author: Fish, Carl Russell, 1876-1932

quoteFirst: Some passers-by have stopped, and some pence have dropped into the old woman's dirty, wasted hand. The man, still drawing his violin bow, looks round, satisfied, on the treasure....
quoteLast: strikes from a steeple near; they suddenly desist, she from her singing, he from the scraping of his miserable instrument, and they go off to swell the little crowd which awaits, at the public-house doors, the sixth stroke of six,--the re-opening of the house where drunkenness, the cure of hunger-pain, is to be cheaply bought.
title: Highways and Byways in London
author: Cook, Emily Constance Baird, -1903; Griggs, Frederick Landseer Maur, 1876-1938 [Illustrator]; Thomson, Hugh, 1860-1920 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: But I hardly think it serious enough for a life calling, so, as you so pleasantly intimate, I believe I will accept your offer to join fortunes with the packing-house. I think I know enough of Latin to decline pig--and I always do when it's our label--but circumstances of a strictly pecuniary nature make it advisable for me to close with you at once. Better an eight-dollar job and
quoteLast: dinner than a post-graduate course and free lunch. While I'm not prepared to admit that my soul soars to the azure at the thought of being a pork packer, perhaps it is just as well.
title:
Letters from a Son to His Self-Made Father
Being the Replies to Letters from a Self-Made Merchant to His Son
author: Merriman, Charles Eustace; Kulz, Fred [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: For four or five miles before we approached the town, the laughing and animated faces of groups of peasantry, all in their jubilee dresses, the old mounted upon asses, and the young walking by the sides of them, hastening to the town, announced to us, that a fair, and merry making was to be held there, on that day. Lisieux was quite in a bustle. About
quoteLast: in the evening of the same day, we arrived at Caen, the capital of Lower Normandy. My fare to this city from Paris, amounted to thirty livres, including my luggage.
title: The stranger in France
author: Carr, John, Sir, 1772-1832

quoteLast: in the evening, precisely, and go on without regard to the old rule of first work and finish, and the day’s work is considered to be completed at the expiration of eleven hours, five o’clock in the morning; if engaged after that time all hands are paid by the hour, the printer never availing himself of the choice of beginning an hour later on account of the lateness of the preceding morning.
title: A dictionary of the art of printing
author: Savage, William, 1770-1843

quoteFirst: I went and drank it, and with all the others lay down thoroughly warm and contented in the long wet grass, and slept soundly for three hours. Next morning we were woken about 3 o’clock, but did not march off till
title: Mons, Anzac and Kut
author: Herbert, Aubrey, 1880-1923
